claim.content: Consider a magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene device that is placed on a hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) substrate with a relative crystallographic alignment angle between the graphene moiré lattice and the hBN lattice denoted by \theta_{\mathrm{rel}}; if \theta_{\mathrm{rel}}>10^{\circ} (ten degrees), then any single-particle sublattice-staggered potential arising from alignment between the moiré superlattice and the hBN lattice is negligible for low-energy electronic states, and consequently the substrate does not impose a significant explicit single-particle twofold rotation (C_{2}) symmetry breaking term in the effective low-energy Hamiltonian of the sample.
